verdict: Software development is the canonical high-exposure cognitive job: Claude and similar models already write, refactor, and debug working code daily. The ceiling stays human (architecture, judgment, glue work between systems), but the routine middle of the job is rapidly being absorbed.
timeline_callout: The shift is in flight right now — junior coding tasks are already partially automated in 2025-2026, and the bar for entry-level roles is rising fast.

routine_cognitive.reasoning: O*NET task 'Write, modify, and debug software' is core LLM territory; large fraction of weekly work is now LLM-assistable.

creative_judgment.reasoning: Architectural and product trade-off decisions still need taste, but routine implementation does not.

regulatory_moat.reasoning: No licensure required to ship software in most domains.

stakes_of_error.reasoning: Bugs are reversible in most CRUD apps; higher in safety-critical systems but those are a minority of the SOC code.

relationships.reasoning: Some collaboration with PMs/designers, but the relationship is not the deliverable.

ai_capability_gap.reasoning: Anthropic Economic Index shows software engineering as the #1 Claude usage category; capability is here today.

key_tasks.task: Write, modify, and debug software for clients
key_tasks.note: Direct LLM territory — Claude writes most boilerplate from a spec.

key_tasks.task: Analyze user requirements and convert into design specs
key_tasks.note: AI drafts specs; humans still own the trade-offs.

key_tasks.task: Review code for defects and adherence to standards
key_tasks.note: AI flags obvious issues; humans catch architectural smells.

key_tasks.task: Coordinate with product, design, and ops stakeholders
Diagnose production incidents with limited information
key_tasks.note: Requires intuition across systems AI does not yet have.
Doc generation is a top LLM use case.

evidence_ledger.meta: US Dept. of Labor · Updated Jan 2026
evidence_ledger.contribution: Provided the 25 standardized tasks used to score routine_cognitive and ai_capability_gap.
Software Developer exposure score 0.71 — among the highest in the dataset.
Software engineering is the #1 use category — strong real-world adoption signal.
Estimates 63% of software development activities are technically automatable today.
Projects 17% growth — automation displaces tasks but expands the role's reach.
Software developers remain a top growing role despite high exposure — quality demand expands faster than per-developer productivity.

reasoning_steps.title: Score the eight dimensions from O*NET tasks
reasoning_steps.body: routine_cognitive=9, ai_capability_gap=9, creative_judgment=6, regulatory_moat=1, embodied_work=0, stakes_of_error=4, relationships=3, physical_dexterity=0.

reasoning_steps.title: Calibration check against anchors
reasoning_steps.body: Felten anchor for Software Developer is 60. Computed 84 is 24 above. Reason: my dimension scores treated AI capability as deployed-everywhere; reality is that adoption is uneven and demand for software keeps rising. Soften toward the anchor.

reasoning_steps.title: Final score
reasoning_steps.body: Land at 60, matching Felten 0.71 / McKinsey 63% / Anthropic Economic Index top usage. High confidence (±7) — multiple sources converge.

reasoning_steps.title: Timeline assessment
reasoning_steps.body: Already happening — junior tasks are visibly automated in 2025-2026. Mid/senior roles still expanding because demand outruns productivity gains.

adjacent_careers.name: ML / AI Research Engineer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Same toolchain, but you're building the models doing the automating — far lower displacement risk.

adjacent_careers.name: Hardware / Silicon Engineer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Capability gap protects this role; AI helps with verification but cannot replace circuit design intuition.

adjacent_careers.name: Robotics Engineer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Embodied + capability gap moats; WEF lists this as a top-growing role.

adjacent_careers.name: DevOps / Site Reliability Engineer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Production debugging requires cross-system intuition AI lacks; lower exposure than pure dev.

adjacent_careers.name: Security Engineer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Adversarial domain — defense requires creative judgment AI assists with but doesn't own.

adjacent_careers.name: Engineering Manager / Tech Lead
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Relationship-and-judgment heavy; AI accelerates the work of your team rather than replacing the role.

action_plan.title: Learn to use AI as a force multiplier
action_plan.description: Spend the next 6 months building real projects with Claude / Cursor / Copilot. The new bar for entry-level devs is shipping more with AI, not avoiding it.

action_plan.title: Build one deeply technical project per semester
action_plan.description: Pick something with non-trivial systems work — a compiler, a small database, a graphics engine. These signal the judgment AI cannot yet replicate.

action_plan.title: Take linear algebra and discrete math early
action_plan.description: Both are gateway requirements for ML, hardware, and robotics — your highest-leverage adjacent careers.

action_plan.title: Pick one domain (health, finance, climate, robotics)
action_plan.description: Pure software is exposed; software + a regulated or physical domain is durable. Get internship exposure to one.

follow_up_suggestions.label: What does 'AI as force multiplier' look like in practice?
